authorities.name: U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

reaches.name: Franktown Creek, Hobart Reservoir to Red House
reaches.toDesc: Red House (former Virginia & Gold Hill Water Company caretaker station) within Lake Tahoe Nevada State Park

sources.title: Franktown Creek — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Region: Western. County: Washoe. Type of water: Creek or Stream.

sources.title: Franktown Creek (GNIS Feature ID 859761) — Washoe County, NV
sources.quotedText: Franktown Creek is located within the Washoe Valley CCD at latitute 39.2752 and longitude -119.8166.

sources.title: Franktown Creek fishing reports — Fishbrain
sources.quotedText: The most common species in the Franktown Creek are: Rainbow trout

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027) — Washoe County, Open Waters
sources.quotedText: WASHOE COUNTY. OPEN WATERS: All waters (Except as noted below). Open year around. Daily: 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish and 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass and 5 may be walleye. Possession: 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish and 30 warmwater gamefish of which 10 may be black bass and 10 may be walleye. Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027) — Washoe County, Hobart Reservoir/Franktown Creek listing
sources.quotedText: Hobart Reservoir and tributaries and Franktown Creek downstream from Hobart Reservoir to Red House. May 1 through October 31. Daily: 5 trout of which not more than 2 may be tiger trout. Possession: 10 trout of which not more than 4 may be tiger trout. 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

parameters.note: Franktown Creek is not separately listed in NBWC CR 25-16's Washoe County waters table except for the segment from Hobart Reservoir downstream to Red House (see the reach-scoped regulations below), so the general Washoe County open-water default applies to the rest of the creek (upstream of Hobart Reservoir and from Red House downstream to Washoe Lake).

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County (Open Waters, except as noted)
regulations.humanSummary: Franktown Creek is open to fishing year-round, except for the reach from Hobart Reservoir downstream to Red House which has its own season (see below).
regulations.verbatimText: All waters (Except as noted below). Open year around.

parameters.counting_rule: General Washoe County open-water limit: 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish, and 15 warmwater game fish daily (of which no more than 5 may be black bass and 5 may be walleye); possession 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish, and 30 warmwater game fish (of which no more than 10 may be black bass and 10 may be walleye). Applies to Franktown Creek outside the separately-regulated Hobart Reservoir-to-Red House reach.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County (Open Waters, except as noted)
regulations.humanSummary: General Washoe County bag limit: 5 trout / 5 mountain whitefish / 15 warmwater game fish daily (max 5 black bass, 5 walleye); possession 10 trout / 10 whitefish / 30 warmwater game fish (max 10 black bass, 10 walleye).
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish and 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass and 5 may be walleye. Possession: 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish and 30 warmwater gamefish of which 10 may be black bass and 10 may be walleye.

parameters.note: No time-of-day restriction on the general (unlisted) portion of Franktown Creek.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County (Open Waters, except as noted)
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is permitted any hour of the day or night on the general (unlisted) portion of Franktown Creek.
regulations.verbatimText: Any hour of the day or night

periods.label: Open season (Hobart Reservoir to Red House reach)

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County, Hobart Reservoir and tributaries and Franktown Creek downstream from Hobart Reservoir to Red House
regulations.humanSummary: The reach of Franktown Creek from Hobart Reservoir downstream to Red House is open May 1 through October 31 only (listed together with Hobart Reservoir and its tributaries).
regulations.verbatimText: Hobart Reservoir and tributaries and Franktown Creek downstream from Hobart Reservoir to Red House. May 1 through October 31.

sub_limits.note: Of the daily/possession trout limit on this reach, only this many may be tiger trout.

parameters.counting_rule: On this reach the full trout limit is 5 daily / 10 in possession, of which no more than 2 daily / 4 in possession may be tiger trout.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County, Hobart Reservoir and tributaries and Franktown Creek downstream from Hobart Reservoir to Red House
regulations.humanSummary: On the Hobart Reservoir-to-Red House reach: daily limit 5 trout (no more than 2 tiger trout); possession limit 10 trout (no more than 4 tiger trout).
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 5 trout of which not more than 2 may be tiger trout. Possession: 10 trout of which not more than 4 may be tiger trout.

parameters.note: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset, on the Hobart Reservoir-to-Red House reach.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County, Hobart Reservoir and tributaries and Franktown Creek downstream from Hobart Reservoir to Red House
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing on the Hobart Reservoir-to-Red House reach is permitted from 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.
regulations.verbatimText: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
